idea.hook: How long to cross our HOME GALAXY?
facts: Our Milky Way galaxy spans about 100,000 light-years across.
Traveling at the speed of light (299,792 km/s), this journey would still take 100,000 years.
The fastest human-made object, Voyager 1 (approx. 17 km/s), would take over 1.7 BILLION years!
idea.payoff: Even at light speed, our galaxy is an UNIMAGINABLY VAST place. We're just a tiny speck!
idea.title: Milky Way Crossing: LIGHT YEARS Ahead! 🤯🌌

scenes.text: How long to cross our HOME GALAXY?
scenes.visual: Smooth zoom out from Earth to show the solar system, then quickly reveal a stylized, awe-inspiring view of the Milky Way galaxy.
Animated infographic of a spiral galaxy with a visible measurement line stretching across its diameter, labeling '100,000 Light-Years'. Stars twinkle gently.

scenes.text: Speed of Light (299,792 km/s): Takes 100,000 YEARS!
scenes.visual: A bright light beam rapidly traverses the galaxy from one end to the other, but a digital counter dramatically displays '100,000 Years' next to it.

scenes.text: Fastest Human-Made: Voyager 1 (17 km/s) = 1.7 BILLION YEARS!
scenes.visual: An animated graphic of a small Voyager probe icon slowly moving across the galaxy, leaving a very faint, long trail, visually emphasizing the glacial pace compared to light.

scenes.text: Our galaxy is UNIMAGINABLY VAST. We are a tiny speck.
scenes.visual: A breathtaking, slow, continuous zoom-out from the entire Milky Way, making it gradually shrink within a larger canvas of countless other galaxies, highlighting our cosmic scale.

script.thumbnail_text: Milky Way: How long to cross?

youtube.title: Milky Way Crossing: LIGHT YEARS Ahead! 🤯🌌
